summary.intent: Build a browser-in-browser feature for moltis, allowing users to view, interact with, and manage sandboxed browser sessions from the settings UI, with session history and cookie persistence.
summary.outcome: Full interactive browser viewer shipped: live CDP screencast, mouse/keyboard/scroll input relay, URL autocomplete bar, session persistence to SQLite, shared cookie profiles, per-agent browser profiles, and session history/action log UI — across ~30 commits on the plan-browser-viewing branch.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService each created independent BrowserManager instances, making tool-created sessions invisible to the UI API — shared via Arc<OnceCell>.
WebSocket broadcast uses an explicit subscription filter (broadcast.rs:100-102) — clients must list event types they want or they silently receive nothing.
axum 0.8 uses {param} syntax for path captures, not :param — runtime panic on startup if old syntax is used.
The moltis.toml config file had explicit viewport/timeout values that silently override code defaults, making default changes ineffective for existing installs.
CDP Page.startScreencast relay task exits immediately on tx.send() with zero receivers — CDP sends the first frame before subscribers connect, causing permanent silence.
CDP mouseWheel requires both deltaX and deltaY to be present even when one is zero — omitting either causes a deserialization error on the Chrome side.
Chrome SingletonLock file in the profile directory prevents reusing profiles across container restarts — must be deleted before launching a new container.
serde #[serde(default)] handles missing JSON keys but NOT explicit null values — LLMs (MiniMax) send null for all optional fields, breaking u64 deserialization.
The canvas useEffect with empty deps runs once on mount; if the canvas is conditionally rendered and not present at mount time, event listeners are never attached — use ref callbacks instead.
CDP screencast metadata includes offset_top (browser chrome height) which must be added to y-coordinates for accurate click mapping.

code.finding: Null-stripping via obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) added before serde deserialization to handle LLM behavior of sending explicit null for all optional fields.
Relay task must not exit on SendError (zero receivers) — changed to drop frames silently and continue waiting for subscribers.
Subscription filter: if !client.is_subscribed_to(event) { continue } — browser.screencast.frame was missing from websocket.js subscription list.
eventHandlers keys are used to build the v4 WebSocket subscription list — adding new event types here is required for them to be received.
start_screencast now calls SetDeviceMetricsOverride to resize viewport to 1280x800@1x before streaming, so pages render for the visible area regardless of configured resolution.
Sandbox sessions now use a shared profile dir (sandbox/default/ or sandbox/{profile_id}/) with SingletonLock cleanup, enabling cookie/session persistence across restarts.
axum route /:session_id panics at startup — must be /{session_id}.
RealBrowserService injects profile_id: 'default' for UI-originated requests and sandbox: true when sandbox mode is enabled, so the UI doesn't need to send these fields.
BrowserRequest now has optional profile_id field; BrowserTool injects the chat session key as profile_id so each agent's sessions share cookies within their own profile.

workflow: Adding tracing logs at every stage of a multi-hop relay chain (CDP → registry → service → WS) is the fastest way to bisect silent data loss bugs.
Integration tests with real browsers should use ephemeral temp profiles and --test-threads=1 to avoid SingletonLock contention between parallel tests.
When removing conditional wrapping from code (an if block), always check for orphaned closing braces — a stray } closed a function early, putting a return at module scope.
replace_all on a field name can accidentally hit unrelated fields with the same name in other structs — prefer targeted edits.

friction: YubiKey tap required for every git push — interrupted the workflow multiple times throughout the session.

Cascading silent failures in the screencast relay: null deserialization, relay exit on zero receivers, missing WebSocket subscription, and missing deltaX/deltaY were all separate bugs that looked identical from the UI (blank canvas).
User config file (moltis.toml) had explicit viewport values overriding code defaults — changing defaults in code had no effect, discovered only after the captcha centering issue persisted.
replace_all on session_store accidentally modified an unrelated GatewayServices field, causing a type mismatch compilation error.
axum 0.8 path syntax change (:param vs {param}) caused a runtime panic only discovered at server startup, not at compile time.
Background task notifications required reading output files, adding friction to the push-verify loop.
Pre-existing clippy errors in moltis-httpd and moltis-voice created noise when running workspace-wide clippy checks.
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko architecture) for sub-100ms latency and 6-18x bandwidth reduction vs CDP JPEG screencast — discussed but deferred.
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast to eliminate ~33% base64 overhead — identified but not implemented.
Multiple concurrent sandbox sessions sharing a profile may have cookie write conflicts — acknowledged tradeoff, no mitigation yet.
Playwright e2e tests exist for browser UI but click/scroll accuracy tests are Rust integration tests requiring a real browser — CI coverage gap for sandbox mode.
Session history UI (closed sessions, action log) was added but screenshot replay of past sessions was not implemented.
URL polling interval (2s) for detecting in-page navigations is a workaround — CDP NavigatedWithinDocument events would be more reliable.
Scrollbar click-to-scroll uses a CDP evaluate call that may fail on CSP-restricted pages.
moltis-httpd has pre-existing compilation errors unrelated to this branch that should be fixed.
